1.text: Choose the statement that best describes the photograph.

choices.A: A lecture is being given in a boardroom.
choices.B: The woman has gotten up from the chair.
choices.C: A diagram is being drawn on the board by the woman.
choices.D: Some documents have been placed on the table.

2.text: Choose the statement that best describes the photograph.

choices.A: Several passengers are getting off the train.
choices.B: The subway has arrived at the station.
choices.C: Tickets are being sold to passengers.
choices.D: There are no seats available in any of the cars.

3.text: Choose the statement that best describes the photograph.

choices.A: Customers are trying on some new shirts in the store.
choices.B: A large number of shirts are being displayed on hangers.
choices.C: The clothes are currently on sale for low prices.
choices.D: A picture is hanging from the wall above the clothes.

4.text: Choose the statement that best describes the photograph.

choices.A: Several participants in the meeting appear to be bored.
choices.B: All of the chairs have been pushed under the table.
choices.C: A computer has been placed in the center of the table.
choices.D: The decision to hold the meeting has been tabled.

5.text: Choose the statement that best describes the photograph.

choices.A: There are several sheep in the field.
choices.B: One of the ships is sailing beneath the bridge.
choices.C: People are getting on board the ship.
choices.D: Pedestrians are walking alongside the waterway.

6.text: Choose the statement that best describes the photograph.

choices.A: The seated people are all facing the same direction.
choices.B: The outdoor dining area at the café is completely full.
choices.C: One man is walking in front of the seated individuals.
choices.D: Waiters are bringing plates of food to the diners.

7.text: How much longer do you need on this project?

8.text: Why hasn't anyone responded to that complaint?

choices.A: That's the response she gave us.
choices.B: Yes, it looks a bit plain to me.
choices.C: I thought Ted already handled it.

9.text: Do you want me to see if the item is still in stock?

choices.A: I would appreciate that a lot.
choices.B: I don't have stock in that company.
choices.C: This is the item that I purchased.

12.text: Nobody can remember Ms. Carlyle's phone number, right?

choices.A: You should ask Jane for it.
choices.C: No, I'm not on the phone.

19.text: Will Martin conduct the interviews, or is John doing them?

choices.A: Actually, Peter will be doing them.

21.text: I can't recall where Ms. Hamilton's office is located.

choices.A: She's in Mr. Wilkin's office now.
choices.B: It's in the building by the Sultan Theater.
choices.C: No, I haven't located her yet.

23.text: How often does the subway come to this station?

choices.A: Five or six times an hour.

25.text: Did you remember to turn off the lights before you left?

choices.A: Yes, I remember what they look like.
choices.C: No, I haven't left the office yet.

30.text: Shouldn't we reserve a car for our trip to Los Angeles?

choices.A: No, I haven't reserved a car yet.
choices.B: We could just take public transportation.
choices.C: On the morning of the thirtieth.

102.text: _____ guarantee the timely delivery of the product, it should be paid for at the time it is purchased.

152.text: In which of the positions marked [1], [2], [3], and [4] does the following sentence best belong? “Some of you will be laid off while others will receive promotions or transfers.”
